content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Four amendments reviewed against source code. Three accepted as real bugs (crash-window duplicate comments, partial mapping loss from coarse fallback guard, wrong br CLI subcommand). One rejected due to factual errors in its analysis (list_payloads already sorts chronologically, call-site guard already exists).

## Votes

- `fr-7-a79f285c`: Accept
  Verified: verify_and_comment_mappings posts comments inside the function (planned_elsewhere.rs:305-333) BEFORE the caller persists the verified record (success_reconciliation.rs:757-786). A crash in that window leaves the mapping unverified in the journal, causing replay to re-verify and re-post the comment. The suggestion to split verification from commenting or make commenting idempotent is sound. Note: this is currently masked by fr-7-d6991eda (wrong br subcommand), but becomes a real issue once that bug is fixed.
- `fr-7-9e0205ad`: Reject
  Two key claims are factually incorrect. First, list_payloads (fs.rs:1107) explicitly calls records.sort_by_key(|r| r.created_at) — ordering IS guaranteed regardless of filesystem listing order, so the 'wrong aggregate' concern is unfounded. Second, the amendment claims 'there is no guard against this reconstruction running multiple times' but the call site (success_reconciliation.rs:722-728) already checks !has_any_for_bead, preventing reconstruction once any mapping exists. Unbounded duplication cannot occur — at most one extra set of events is written. The remaining concern (journal cleanliness) is acknowledged by the amendment itself as not a correctness bug and is too minor to warrant a code change.
- `fr-7-3afb6840`: Accept
  Verified: the fallback guard (success_reconciliation.rs:722) checks has_any_for_bead — a boolean across ALL rounds. If round 1 successfully records mapping A but round 2 fails to record mapping B (since engine.rs:2697-2712 records as best-effort post-commit), the fallback never triggers because mapping A already exists. Additionally, reconstruct_pe_mappings_from_aggregate only examines the latest final-review aggregate, so even if it were called, it would miss mappings from earlier rounds. This is a real correctness bug: accepted planned-elsewhere findings can be permanently lost.
- `fr-7-d6991eda`: Accept
  Verified: br_process.rs:227-229 builds BrCommand with Self::new("comment").arg(id).arg(text), producing `br comment <id> <text>`. The actual br CLI accepts `br comments add <id> <text>` (confirmed via bootstrap script docs/ralph_milestone_beads_bootstrap_v2.sh:73). This means every comment_bead call in planned_elsewhere.rs:305-312 fails at runtime, and mapped-to beads never receive context comments. Clear, straightforward bug with a simple fix: change Self::new("comment") to Self::new("comments").arg("add").
